description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves create a striped pattern, alternating between raised stone and recessed channels, which may serve both functional and aesthetic purposes. The block is solid and robust, with clean, sharp edges and a uniform, dark coloration, suggesting it is made from a dense stone such as granite or basalt. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, contrasting with the textured top. Its substantial thickness and mass indicate it is designed to act as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, possibly to control pedestrian or vehicular movement. The overall appearance is modern and utilitarian, suitable for integration into cityscapes or public infrastructure.
description: A solid, rectangular stone block with a grooved, striped top surface and smooth, dark sides. It appears heavy, durable, and designed for use as an urban barrier or obstacle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the depth of the grooves and the block's thickness.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the striped pattern from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view, again emphasizing the depth and regularity of the grooves.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a traffic or pedestrian barrier
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with sharp edges.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
